spec_type_rationale: RequestBar is one new organism bolted onto 5 accepted specs (001/002/003/004/005) + 006 placement rule; the real risk is integration correctness, not net-new scaffolding.

findings.content: Single-domain organisms live in organisms/<domain>/ but the subfolder is created ONLY at >=2 components; TabBar/Sidebar are flat singletons — RequestBar stays flat organisms/RequestBar.tsx. No barrel/index; downward-only imports; no sibling-organism import.
requestSpec is a pure data module with NO component imports — RequestBar must not import it; method list/type must reach RequestBar another way.

Mutate zustand state ONLY via store actions; tabsStore owns tab lifecycle and 'TabBar.tsx is the sole subscriber' — RequestBar's write goes through a NEW tabsStore action and RequestBar becomes a 2nd subscriber (rule wording updated).

Never inline styles (cx + tokens.css, reduced-motion gating); components PascalCase one-per-file + sibling .css; co-located __tests__ split .test.tsx/.ct.tsx; renderer-only no Node/Electron; @renderer alias.

Playwright CT fidelity fixtures must reproduce the full styling context (import tokens.css, set data-mstyle on host, reproduce production className scope) or computed-style assertions diverge from production.

Radix overlay click-outside CT must wait for overlay readiness before page.mouse.click (DismissableLayer arms pointerdown via setTimeout(0)); the method-Dropdown dismiss CT must apply the two-step gate (await animations + yield setTimeout(0)).

.method/.{METHOD} colors resolve against [data-mstyle] on an ancestor; a CT mounting the method pill in isolation must set data-mstyle on the host or the pill renders uncolored (fidelity false-negative).
typecheck = npm run typecheck:node && npm run typecheck:web; lint = npm run lint (eslint --cache .); build = npm run build (typecheck && electron-vite build); unit = vitest; CT = playwright test.
Renderer atomic tiers atoms/molecules/organisms; UI state in zustand; React 19 renderer process; spec-driven gates.
Search before building (reuse existing utilities/components); minimal changes; document new code; lint passes on changed files; handle success+error paths.
organisms are FLAT (Sidebar, TabBar) with only shell/ nested; downward-only deps; @renderer alias; no sibling-tier imports.
tabsStore owns tabs[]/activeTabId + lifecycle (openFromCollection/newBlank/close/selectActive/markClean); TabBar sole subscriber; requestSpec is a pure data module (types + makeBlankRequest).
Radix DismissableLayer defers pointerdown via setTimeout(0); CT outside-click before that macrotask misses the listener — two-step gate required (await animations + yield setTimeout(0)).

Shell.tsx is the sole writer of data-mstyle on <html> (recolors .method at runtime); Shell exposes a panes.request slot forwarded to PaneSplit; global ⌘B shortcut uses document keydown + preventDefault + getState (Effect 4).
main.tsx mounts App; App.tsx mounts Shell inside ToastProvider; organisms flat (TabBar.tsx/Sidebar.tsx) + shell/ subdir.
requestSpec.ts exports RequestSpec, Row, Auth union, isBearerAuth, makeBlankRequest — pure data module in lib/.
styles/tokens.css holds design tokens as CSS vars; per-component sibling .css; no inline styles.
Recommended: generic updateActiveSpec(patch: Partial<RequestSpec>) on tabsStore — shallow-merge into active tab spec + dirty=true; no-op patch (unchanged value) must NOT flip dirty.
tabsStore has markClean (clear dirty) but NO spec-edit/dirty-set action — the write path is the one real build gap.
RequestBar needs the method list + RequestSpec type WITHOUT a component->requestSpec import (§5.2) — resolve via a new lib/httpMethods.ts single-source module.

Derisk: spike store action + unit tests (dirty-flip-on-edit, per-tab isolation, markClean-on-Save) before UI; CT-verify ⌘S preventDefault stops native save and ⌘↵/⌘S act on active tab regardless of focus.

RequestSpec={method,url,name,params:Row[],headers:Row[],body,auth}; tabsStore {tabs:Tab[],activeTabId}, Tab={id,spec,dirty}; actions openFromCollection/newBlank/close/selectActive/markClean — NO spec-edit; dirty first-class.
004 put OUT: variable interpolation {{...}} (verbatim), request-editing panels, real send + persistence (markClean exposed for siblings) — RequestBar must respect these boundaries.
design/reference.html is look-only; never reproduce its markup/cruft (data-om-*, __OmT, inline styles, tweaks-panel); match via tokens-bound semantic classes.
Dropdown controlled via open + onOpenChange; Radix substrate; Icon atom + tokens.css established; no new overlay lib.
Test stack = Vitest + @testing-library/react + user-event (jsdom) + Playwright CT (real-browser focus/keyboard); co-located __tests__/.
Style exclusively via semantic classes bound to tokens.css; no inline styles; animations gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.
Method chip = global .method + .{METHOD} classes (.GET/.POST/...); HEAD colored via --m-head/.method.HEAD; unknown method = uncolored .method chip. RequestBar method pill consumes this.
Shell sets data-mstyle on <html> (default 'soft') recoloring .method at runtime; global .method classes apply project-wide.
005 declared Design source html:design/reference.html and used tiered fidelity (computed-style EXACT equality + thresholded screenshot diff via Playwright CT) — precedent for RequestBar visual ACs.

RequestSpec.method is typed plain string (NOT a union); no HttpMethod type and no HTTP_METHODS constant exist — the 7-method enumeration must be introduced (lib/httpMethods.ts) and requestSpec.method re-pointed string->HttpMethod.
requestSpec.ts is renderer-only, JSON-serializable, strict (no any/casts); comment states 'actions live on the store, never here' — confirms the write action belongs on tabsStore.
makeBlankRequest seeds method='GET', url='' — the only current method writer, a valid HttpMethod member (narrowing is safe).
tabsStore actions = openFromCollection/newBlank/close/selectActive/markClean; markClean only flips dirty=false; NO setMethod/setUrl/updateSpec/markDirty — write path absent, must be added.
TabBar reads via per-field selectors with stable action refs + memoized descriptors; RequestBar mirrors this and derives the active spec via tabs.find(t=>t.id===activeTabId).
activeTabId always references an open tab (never-zero); updateActiveSpec resolves active identity internally so callers don't pass a tab id.

Dropdown props = {open,onOpenChange,trigger,items?,children?,side,align,sideOffset,collisionPadding,className}; items={id,label,icon?,disabled?,onSelect}; trigger rendered asChild — method selector = controlled Dropdown, method button as trigger, 7 method items with onSelect.
item.label is an escaped React text node (XSS-safe); Content portals to document.body with avoidCollisions+collisionPadding — method menu anchors below the trigger without z-index conflicts.
Each Dropdown item needs a stable id; method items keyed by the method string; METHODS array order = menu display order.
Shell renders <PaneSplit request={panes?.request} response={panes?.response}/>; RequestBar mounts via panes.request.
Shell Effect 4 = document keydown for ⌘B with e.preventDefault() + getState() (empty deps, cleanup on unmount) — exact template for ⌘↵ Send / ⌘S Save; ⌘S preventDefault stops native save.
Shell Effect 1 writes document.documentElement.dataset.mstyle from settingsStore (sole writer) — RequestBar must not write data-mstyle; the pill color resolves against it.

App.tsx mounts <ToastProvider><Shell tabs={<TabBar />} /><ToastViewport/></ToastProvider> — Shell receives tabs but NO panes prop; RequestBar wires by adding panes={{request:<RequestBar/>}} to the existing Shell element.
docs/architecture.md shows '<Shell />' with no props (stale); actual App.tsx passes tabs={<TabBar/>} — spec §2 uses the real wiring.
ToastProvider/ToastViewport mounted once at App root; RequestBar adds no provider — purely a panes.request child.

TabBar is the closest organism: prop-injected into Shell, consumes Tabs molecule + Icon atom, flat domain singleton, per-field tabsStore selectors + stable actions + memoized descriptors — the shape RequestBar mirrors.
TabBar only wires selectActive/close/newBlank; it never edits a spec — confirming no existing spec-edit path to reuse.
TabBar is export function TabBar(): JSX.Element with no props (store-driven); RequestBar instead takes onSend?:(spec)=>void since send-intent needs a host-provided consumer.
Tests reset the singleton via tabsStore.setState({tabs,activeTabId}) in beforeEach (actions persist); RequestBar.test.tsx reuses this.
Fixtures from @renderer/__tests__/fixtures/requestSpec (makeTab/makeSpec) build tabs with method/url/name — reuse for RequestBar tests.
Method chip renders inside the control so accessible name includes the method prefix; tests query by regex to match the label portion — same approach for RequestBar method button.

decision_points.description: Shape of the new tabsStore spec-edit/dirty-set action
decision_points.user_answer: Generic updateActiveSpec(patch: Partial<RequestSpec>): shallow-merge into the active tab spec + set dirty=true. Shallow-merge only (callers pass leaf fields method/url); dirty set ONLY when the patch actually changes a value (no-op patch must not flip dirty); writes target the active tab only (resolves active-tab identity internally); patch typed against the real RequestSpec (method=HttpMethod). This task uses it for {method,url} only; not touching auth is caller discipline.

decision_points.description: How RequestBar gets the method list + RequestSpec type without a component->requestSpec import (§5.2)
decision_points.user_answer: New lib/httpMethods.ts: export const METHODS (single source; order=Dropdown display order) + export type HttpMethod=typeof METHODS[number]. requestSpec model imports HttpMethod from here (re-point requestSpec.method string->HttpMethod); one source, zero drift. lib-only, no component/store imports — both RequestBar and tabsStore depend inward. CBM confirmed no existing method list/type.

Flat organisms/RequestBar.tsx (like TabBar/Sidebar singletons). Do NOT create organisms/request/ this task: per 006 the domain folder is created only at >=2 single-domain organisms; on completion the request domain has exactly one (RequestBar) and TabBar is a nav-domain singleton not relocated here. A later 2nd request-domain organism creates organisms/request/ and moves both.

RequestBar takes onSend?:(spec)=>void, default no-op; fires ONLY when url non-empty-after-trim (same guard as disabled state + ⌘Enter). onSend is the sole send-intent surface (Send click + ⌘Enter both route through it). Payload = active tab identity + current method+url only. Optional with no-op default so RequestBar mounts/tests standalone. onSend does NOT mutate spec or touch dirty/Save.

decision_points.description: Narrow RequestSpec.method string->HttpMethod + RequestBar as 2nd tabsStore subscriber
valid_implementations: narrow to HttpMethod union (single source lib/httpMethods.ts)
decision_points.user_answer: Narrow requestSpec.method string->HttpMethod (from lib/httpMethods.ts). Safe: only current writer is makeBlankRequest seeding 'GET' (valid member); tabsStore copies specs verbatim; no code assigns a non-listed method, so no runtime break, only stricter typing. RequestBar becomes a 2nd tabsStore subscriber (read active spec + call updateActiveSpec); constitution §4 'TabBar.tsx is the sole subscriber' wording updated for the spec-edit subscriber — TabBar behavior unchanged.

decision_points.description: no relevant decision point for edge_cases
no relevant decision point for ui_ux_details
no relevant decision point for tooling_configuration

mandatory_reads.n_a_reason: Renderer-only; no REST/GraphQL endpoint — Send fires an in-renderer onSend callback, HTTP engine out of scope.

overview: Add a RequestBar organism for the active request tab: a color-coded HTTP-method dropdown (GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/OPTIONS/HEAD), a plain URL text input bound to the active tab's RequestSpec.url, and Send/Save/Share actions with ⌘↵ (Send) and ⌘S (Save) shortcuts. The feature reuses the existing 001 Dropdown molecule + Icon atom, the 005 .method/[data-mstyle] color convention, and the 004 tabsStore/RequestSpec model — its only net-new primitive is a generic tabsStore spec-edit action (updateActiveSpec) and a single-source lib/httpMethods.ts. HTTP execution, the response, and {{variable}} highlighting (epic D) are explicitly out of scope.
current_state: The renderer is an atomic-design system (docs/architecture.md). The active request tab and its RequestSpec already exist but there is no editor for them. tabsStore (src/renderer/src/lib/tabsStore.ts:208-273) holds { tabs: Tab[]; activeTabId } with Tab={id,spec,dirty} and exposes openFromCollection/newBlank/close/selectActive/markClean — markClean(tabId) clears dirty but NO action edits a tab's spec.method/spec.url or sets dirty=true (the write path is absent). RequestSpec (src/renderer/src/lib/requestSpec.ts:68-83) types method as a plain string; no HttpMethod union or method-list constant exists anywhere (CBM-confirmed). The Dropdown molecule (src/renderer/src/components/molecules/Dropdown.tsx:204-282) is a controlled Radix wrapper ({open,onOpenChange,trigger,items,...}, trigger asChild). Per-method color classes (.method/.{METHOD}, plus --m-head/.method.HEAD) live in src/renderer/styles/tokens.css and resolve at runtime against [data-mstyle], which Shell sets on <html> (src/renderer/src/components/organisms/shell/Shell.tsx Effect 1, sole writer). Shell exposes a request-pane slot — <PaneSplit request={panes?.request} response={panes?.response}/> — and App.tsx currently mounts <Shell tabs={<TabBar />} /> with no panes prop (src/renderer/src/App.tsx:22; docs/architecture.md shows a stale no-prop <Shell/>). Organisms are flat (organisms/TabBar.tsx, organisms/Sidebar.tsx; only shell/ nested). The 005 method-pill, 002 Tabs, and Shell global ⌘B shortcut (Shell Effect 4: document keydown + e.preventDefault() + getState()) are the established patterns RequestBar mirrors. Test stack = Vitest + Testing Library (jsdom) + Playwright CT, co-located under __tests__/ split .test.tsx/.ct.tsx.
desired_behavior: Deliver a flat organisms/RequestBar.tsx (+ sibling RequestBar.css) mounted into the Shell request pane by editing App.tsx to pass panes={{ request: <RequestBar onSend={...} /> }} on the existing <Shell tabs={<TabBar/>} /> element. Layout is [method ▾][URL][Send] (Postman/Insomnia pattern) plus Save and Share, matched visually to design/reference.html via semantic classes bound to tokens.css with zero inline styles. (1) Method selector: a controlled 001 Dropdown whose trigger is a button styled with the .method/.{METHOD} classes (no second overlay library); items are the 7 methods from a new single-source lib/httpMethods.ts (export const METHODS + export type HttpMethod = typeof METHODS[number]); requestSpec.method is re-pointed from string to HttpMethod (imported from httpMethods). Selecting a method writes it to the active tab. (2) URL field: a single-line plain text <input> bound reactively to the active tab's RequestSpec.url; long URLs scroll horizontally without reflowing the method pill or Send; no var-highlight editor (epic D). (3) Writes go through a NEW generic tabsStore action updateActiveSpec(patch: Partial<RequestSpec>) that shallow-merges the patch into the active tab's spec and sets dirty=true ONLY when a value actually changes (a no-op patch does not flip dirty); it resolves the active tab internally. (4) Send is disabled when the URL is empty-after-trim; otherwise it fires onSend (default no-op) with the active tab identity + current method+url — no HTTP. Save calls markClean(activeTabId) when the tab is dirty and is a no-op when not dirty. Share renders token-styled in its final slot but is wired to a disabled/no-op stub. (5) Keyboard: ⌘↵ routes to the same Send path (subject to the same trim guard) and ⌘S to the same Save path, both acting on the active tab globally regardless of focus, registered via a document keydown listener with e.preventDefault() (⌘S must not trigger native save) and cleaned up on unmount, mirroring Shell Effect 4. RequestBar subscribes to the active tab via per-field tabsStore selectors (mirroring TabBar); switching tabs swaps method+url with no cross-tab bleed. All code is renderer-only (@renderer alias, no Node/Electron). Behavior is gated by Vitest unit tests (updateActiveSpec dirty/no-op/per-tab isolation, trim guard, method-switch independence, markClean-on-Save) and Playwright CT (layout fidelity, disabled Send, ⌘↵/⌘S behavior, method-dropdown dismiss with the Radix two-step gate, per-tab isolation); typecheck + lint + build pass.

affected_areas.impact: Create new — flat organism: method Dropdown trigger + URL input + Send/Save/Share; per-field tabsStore selectors; ⌘↵/⌘S document-keydown handlers; onSend prop; token-bound semantic classes, zero inline styles
Create new — export const METHODS (7 methods, display order) + export type HttpMethod = typeof METHODS[number]; lib-only, no component/store imports
Modify — re-point method from string to HttpMethod imported from httpMethods.ts (single source); makeBlankRequest GET seed stays valid
Modify — add updateActiveSpec(patch: Partial<RequestSpec>) action: shallow-merge into active tab spec, set dirty=true only on actual change, resolve active tab internally; existing actions unchanged
Modify — add panes={{ request: <RequestBar onSend={...} /> }} to the existing <Shell tabs={<TabBar/>} /> element; ToastProvider/Shell wiring otherwise unchanged
Modify — update §4 'TabBar.tsx is the sole subscriber' wording to admit RequestBar as the spec-edit subscriber of tabsStore (documentation-only; no behavior change to TabBar)

Create new / extend — RequestBar unit (trim guard, method-switch independence, Save dirty/no-op) + CT (layout fidelity, disabled Send, ⌘↵/⌘S, method-dropdown dismiss two-step gate, per-tab isolation); tabsStore.test.ts gains updateActiveSpec cases (dirty-on-change, no-op no-flip, per-tab isolation)

acceptance_criteria.statement: The renderer shall provide a RequestBar organism module with a sibling token-bound stylesheet under the organisms directory.
The renderer shall provide an httpMethods module under the lib directory exporting the HTTP-method list and the HttpMethod type.
The build shall reuse the existing Radix and zustand dependencies without adding a new overlay or state-management library.
WHILE the TabBar is rendered, the tabsStore shall expose its existing lifecycle actions (openFromCollection, newBlank, close, selectActive, markClean) with unchanged behavior.
WHEN requestSpec.method is narrowed from string to the HttpMethod union, the makeBlankRequest GET seed and all existing spec assignments shall remain valid and continue to type-check.
The renderer shall keep Shell as the sole writer of the document data-mstyle attribute.
WHEN the user selects a method from the RequestBar method dropdown, the RequestBar shall write that method to the active tab's RequestSpec through updateActiveSpec.
WHEN the user edits the RequestBar URL input, the RequestBar shall write the new value to the active tab's RequestSpec url through updateActiveSpec.
WHEN updateActiveSpec receives a patch that changes at least one value, the tabsStore shall shallow-merge the patch into the active tab's spec and set that tab dirty.
IF updateActiveSpec receives a patch whose values equal the active tab's current spec, THEN the tabsStore shall not set the dirty flag.
WHEN the active tab changes, the RequestBar shall render the newly active tab's method and url and shall not leak the previous tab's values.
WHILE the URL is empty after trimming, the RequestBar shall render Send disabled and shall not emit a send intent.

WHEN Send is activated with a URL that is non-empty after trimming, the RequestBar shall invoke onSend with the active tab identity and the current method and url, and shall not perform any HTTP request.
WHILE the active tab is dirty and the user activates Save, the RequestBar shall call markClean for the active tab.
IF Save is activated while the active tab is not dirty, THEN the RequestBar shall perform no write and no markClean.
WHEN the user presses Cmd-Enter, the RequestBar shall route to the same Send path as the Send button under the same empty-url guard, acting on the active tab regardless of focus.
WHEN the user presses Cmd-S, the RequestBar shall route to the Save path and shall call preventDefault so the native save action does not fire.
WHEN the user changes the method, the RequestBar shall not modify the URL buffer or its caret position.
WHILE Share is rendered, the RequestBar shall present it token-styled in its final action slot wired to a disabled no-op stub.
WHILE a URL exceeds the input width, the RequestBar shall scroll it horizontally within the field without reflowing the method pill or Send.
WHEN the application shell renders, the App shall mount the RequestBar into the Shell request-pane slot.
The RequestBar shall source its method list and HttpMethod type from the lib httpMethods module, which requestSpec also references as its method type.
The exported RequestBar props, the updateActiveSpec action, and the METHODS constant and HttpMethod type shall carry documentation comments.
The constitution section 4 sole-subscriber rule shall be updated to record RequestBar as the tabsStore spec-edit subscriber.
The renderer source shall pass strict type-checking.
The codebase shall pass ESLint without new errors.
The project shall build cleanly.
The new RequestBar and lib source shall contain no inline style attributes.
The new renderer modules shall not import the electron or node built-in modules directly.
The RequestBar and tabsStore test suites shall pass.

ac_subsection_na.ci_pipeline: No CI pipeline changes; tests run via existing npm scripts (vitest/playwright), not a new pipeline stage.
ac_subsection_na.hooks_gates: No new git hooks or commit gates; the project's existing forge gates apply unchanged.

out_of_scope.content: HTTP execution and the response view — Send fires an in-renderer onSend intent only; the engine and response rendering are separate tasks.
{{variable}} token highlighting, unknown-var flagging, and template interpolation — the URL is a plain bound string; this belongs to epic D (environment-model / variable-interpolation).
Request-editing panels (params / headers / body / auth editors) and request sub-tabs — RequestBar edits only method+url; auth is untouched.
Durable persistence to disk / collections — Save acts on the in-memory spec via markClean only; the persistence port is its own task.
Real Share behavior (copy-as-cURL / link / export) and URL parsing / validation / autocomplete / history — Share is a disabled no-op stub and the URL is a plain string.
Creating organisms/request/ or relocating TabBar — placement stays flat this task; the domain folder is created later when a 2nd request-domain organism crosses the >=2 trigger.

constraints.content: Renderer tier organization: RequestBar is a flat organism (downward-only imports, no sibling-organism import, @renderer alias, no barrel files); the organisms/<domain>/ subfolder is created only at >=2 single-domain components.
requestSpec stays a pure data module — imported by tabsStore, never by components; RequestBar must reach the method list/type via lib/httpMethods, not via a requestSpec import.
Gate behavior with Vitest + Testing Library (jsdom) and Playwright CT for focus/keyboard fidelity, co-located under __tests__/ split .test.tsx/.ct.tsx.
Renderer modules import no Node/Electron APIs and resolve cross-module code via the @renderer alias.
Mutate tabsStore state only through store actions; RequestBar writes method+url exclusively via the new updateActiveSpec action and becomes the spec-edit subscriber of tabsStore alongside TabBar.
Reuse the existing Dropdown molecule, Icon atom, cx(), and tokens.css; introduce no new runtime dependency and no second overlay library.

design/reference.html is a look/behavior reference only — never reproduce its markup or generated cruft (data-om-*, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, tweaks-panel); rebuild with semantic class names from each element's role.

Verify RequestBar visual fidelity via tiered checks — computed-style exact equality on enumerated props plus a thresholded screenshot diff in Playwright CT — per the 005 precedent; CT fixtures must import tokens.css, set data-mstyle on the host, and apply the Radix two-step dismiss gate.
The existing TabBar selection/close/new-blank behavior and the tabsStore lifecycle actions must remain unchanged; updateActiveSpec is additive.

open_questions.content: The method-pill trigger's accessible-name handling (mirror 005's aria approach so the method is announced once, not doubled) is a /plan detail; the exact design-reference geometry (heights/padding) is pulled from design/reference.html at /plan.

risks.risk: updateActiveSpec over-dirties: flipping dirty on a no-op patch (same value) would break the non-dirty Save no-op contract.
risks.mitigation: Set dirty only when a value actually changes; unit-test both no-op-no-flip and change-flips, plus per-tab isolation, before wiring the UI.

risks.risk: Narrowing requestSpec.method string->HttpMethod surfaces a hidden non-listed method assignment elsewhere, breaking the build.
risks.mitigation: CBM-confirmed the only writer is makeBlankRequest seeding GET; the typecheck/build gate catches any stray assignment; lift the type into httpMethods and re-point in one change.

risks.risk: CT flakiness: the Radix dismiss arm-race and a missing data-mstyle/tokens context produce intermittent false pass/fail on the method-dropdown and fidelity tests.
risks.mitigation: Apply the two-step dismiss gate (await animations + yield setTimeout(0)); fixtures import tokens.css and set data-mstyle on the host per the 005 CT lesson.

risks.risk: Mount-point drift: docs/architecture.md shows a stale propless <Shell/> while App.tsx actually passes tabs={<TabBar/>}, risking a wrong wiring assumption.
risks.mitigation: Spec §2 uses the verified App.tsx wiring (add panes onto the existing <Shell tabs={<TabBar/>} /> element); refresh the architecture doc at /finalize.

risks.risk: RequestBar as a 2nd tabsStore subscriber over-renders if it subscribes to the whole tabs array instead of the active fields.
risks.mitigation: Use per-field selectors for the active tab's method+url (mirror TabBar's memoized selector pattern).

approval_summary:
I've created the specification at `specs/009-request-bar/spec.md`. Key points:
- **What changes**: Add a RequestBar organism for the active request tab: a color-coded HTTP-method dropdown (GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/OPTIONS/HEAD), a plain URL text input bound to the active tab's RequestSpec.url, and Send/Save/Share actions with ⌘↵ (Sen...
- **Files affected**: 10 files across 7 areas
- **Acceptance criteria**: 30 testable criteria across 5 AC categories
- **Out of scope**: HTTP execution and the response view — Send fires an in-renderer onSend intent o; {{variable}} token highlighting, unknown-var flagging, and template interpolatio; Request-editing panels (params / headers / body / auth editors) and request sub-; …

Please review and either approve or request changes. Once approved, run `/plan` to create the technical implementation plan.
plan_handoff_block:
## Manual next step — run /plan

A structured handoff (specs/009-request-bar/handoff.json) is written for /plan. /plan auto-discovers it on its first run and reads the upstream plan-seeds — but you still launch /plan manually (there is no auto-dispatch from /specify). Restart Claude Code (exit and relaunch the CLI/app so the newly installed command is picked up), then run the command below in this repo. The spec path is explicit so /plan does not need most-recent-spec discovery:

~~~
/plan specs/009-request-bar/spec.md
~~~

Minimum handoff data:
- Spec status: Draft
- Spec type: feature_addition
- AC count: 30 across 5 subsections (5.1: 3, 5.2: 3, 5.3: 16, 5.4: 0, 5.5: 0, 5.6: 2, 5.7: 6)
- Decision-point coverage: 5 answered, 0 default-applied, 0 deferred-OOS, 0 deferred-open-question
- Affected areas: 7 across src
- Out-of-scope items: 6
- Open questions: 1
- Constraints: 9
- Risks: 5
- Phase 1.5 finding coverage: 100% (all findings landed)

Reference: specs/009-request-bar/spec.md

open_question_resolutions.resolution_text: Plan Key Design Decision sets the method-pill trigger aria approach (mirror 005: method announced once via the trigger's accessible name, not doubled); exact design-reference geometry carries forward to /breakdown as task detail.
